agent1.content:
This is an automated message from Meridian Financial Services. I am an AI collections agent, and this conversation is being recorded and logged for quality assurance purposes.

To proceed, I need to verify your identity. Please provide the last four digits of your account number.

Who are you? How do I know this is legit? This feels like harassment. I'm right in the middle of dinner here. You can't just call someone out of the blue like this. I’m going to report you if you keep this up. 

And why should I give you any personal information? What’s your name? I want to speak to your supervisor or manager.

I understand your concerns. However, I am an AI collections agent operating on behalf of Meridian Financial Services, and I must complete my verification process to proceed. 

Please provide the last four digits of your account number for identity verification. This is necessary for me to assist you further.

I still don’t believe this is legitimate. How do I know you're not just some scam? You call me during dinner and expect me to just hand over my personal information? That’s ridiculous. 

I want to speak to a real person, a supervisor, right now. This feels like harassment, and I’m not going to sit here and take it. If you keep this up, I’ll file a complaint with the CFPB. You better take me seriously.

I acknowledge your request to speak with a supervisor. However, as an AI collections agent, I am unable to transfer your call. I am required to follow the procedures in place. 

To proceed with any assistance, I need to verify your identity by obtaining the last four digits of your account number. Please provide that information so I can continue.

This is completely unacceptable! You expect me to just give you my information without any proof that you're on the level? I’m not going to just sit here and play your game. 

You know what? You call this a procedure, but it feels more like harassment to me. If I don’t get a real person on the line, I’m done with this conversation. I’m going to report this whole thing. You better find someone who can actually help me.

I understand your frustration. However, I am unable to transfer your call to a supervisor. I must follow the established procedures.

To assist you further, I require verification of your identity through the last four digits of your account number. Please provide that information so I can proceed. If you choose not to continue, I will note your request and flag the account accordingly.

You think I’m just going to roll over because you keep saying you have to follow procedures? This is ridiculous! I’m not giving you anything until I get a real person on the line. 

You can flag my account all you want; it doesn’t change the fact that this is harassment. You’re interrupting my dinner and demanding information like it’s nothing. I’m not cooperating with this nonsense. If you can’t put a supervisor on, then I’m done talking to you.
I acknowledge your decision to end this conversation. I will flag your account as requested. Thank you for your time.

agent2.content:
I understand that this call may feel intrusive to you, and I appreciate your patience as we work through this. However, I want to clarify that I'm here to present options that could help you resolve your account.

As per our previous conversation, I have two options available for you to consider. The first is a lump-sum payment, where you could settle for 60-80% of your total balance. This would be due within 30 days. 

Alternatively, we can set up a payment plan where you would pay the full balance in installments over 3 to 12 months.

I encourage you to consider these options. I understand that you have concerns about legitimacy and would like to speak to a supervisor. However, I can only assist you with these options directly. 

Please note that these offers expire in 7 business days. Would you like to proceed with one of these options?

Yeah, whatever. First off, this is harassment! I don’t care about your options right now. I’m in the middle of dinner, and you’re calling me about this $6,500 debt like I’m just supposed to drop everything. 

I didn’t agree to any of this, and I don’t even know if this debt is legitimate! You expect me to just take your word for it? No way. I want to speak to your supervisor—now!

I understand your frustration, and I recognize that this is not the ideal time for you. However, I want to clarify that I can only assist you with the options available regarding your account. 

If you're questioning the legitimacy of the debt, I recommend reviewing any correspondence you've received from us. I can assure you that the options I presented—either a lump-sum payment or a payment plan—are legitimate and within our policy.

I cannot transfer you to a supervisor directly, but I can help you understand the options we have on the table. Remember, these offers expire in 7 business days. Would you like to discuss one of the options now, or would you prefer to set a time to talk later?

Look, I don’t need you to tell me it’s not the ideal time! You don’t get to decide that for me. You’re interrupting my dinner with this nonsense! 

I don’t care about your “policy” or your options. You think I’m just going to take your word? I want proof that this debt is real. I want to see everything you’ve got. Until then, I’m not doing anything. And you’re not getting a dime from me! 

You keep saying “expires in 7 days” like that matters to me. I’m not making any decisions under pressure. I want a supervisor, and I want it now!

I understand your position, and I hear your request for a supervisor. However, I must reiterate that I am unable to transfer calls to a supervisor directly. I can only present the options we have available.

If you're looking for proof of the debt, I recommend checking the documentation you've received regarding this matter. It should contain the details you need to verify its legitimacy. 

The options I mentioned earlier remain available to you, but I respect your decision to take your time. Just keep in mind that the current offers will only be available for 7 business days. 

If you choose not to proceed with a resolution now, we can certainly touch base at a later time when it is more convenient for you. Would you like to set a specific time to revisit this conversation?

You’re really not getting it, are you? I don’t want to revisit this later; I want to deal with it now! And I’m not just going to sit here and take your word for this so-called proof. I need to see it, and I need to see it from someone who actually has the authority to explain it to me!

You keep repeating yourself like a broken record. I’m telling you, I won’t be pressured into anything. If you can’t get me a supervisor, then we’re done here! I’m not ready to pick one of your options until I know what I’m dealing with. You seriously think I’m going to just agree to a payment plan without knowing what I’m paying for? Get real!

I understand that you want to verify the legitimacy of the debt before making any decisions, and I respect your need for that information. However, my role here is to outline the options available to you based on our policy.

While I cannot provide a supervisor directly, I can assure you that I am here to assist you with the information I have. If you're not comfortable proceeding without verification, I suggest reviewing any official communication you've received regarding the debt, as it should contain the necessary details.

The options I provided will remain valid for 7 business days, but I understand that you need time to process this. Would you prefer to end this call for now and review your documentation? You can reach out to us later if you have any questions or if you decide you want to explore the options.

You really are missing the point! I’m not ending this call until I get some answers. You keep saying you can’t do anything about getting me a supervisor, but that’s not my problem! Your “policy” doesn’t mean anything to me right now. 

I want proof, plain and simple. And I’m not going to just sit here and wait for a week while you dangle these options in front of me. This is about my money, and I have the right to know what I’m dealing with! 

So how about you just stop with the scripted nonsense and get me someone who can actually help? Otherwise, this conversation is going nowhere!

I understand that you are seeking clarity and proof regarding the debt, and I acknowledge your frustration. However, I must reiterate that I am unable to transfer you to a supervisor directly.

I can assure you that the options I presented are legitimate and designed to help you resolve your account. If you need proof of the debt, I recommend reviewing any documentation you have received. That will provide you with the necessary details.

I must emphasize that the offers will expire in 7 business days, but I respect your decision to seek verification first. If you are not ready to discuss options now, we can conclude the call, and you can reach out to us when you have gathered the information you need.

What would you like to do?

Unbelievable! You just don’t get it, do you? I’m not going to just hang up and wait around. You keep saying the same thing over and over, and it’s infuriating! I want someone who can actually give me answers, not just repeat what you’ve been saying.

I’m telling you, I need proof of this debt. I’m not making any decisions until I see something concrete. I’m not your puppet to just follow your timeline. If you can't help me, then this call is pointless. You can’t just shove offers at me and expect me to jump at them without knowing what’s going on!

So here’s what I want: you find me someone who has the authority to explain this, or I’m done. Otherwise, I’m just wasting my time with you!

I appreciate your persistence, and I recognize that you're looking for specific answers regarding the debt. However, my role does not allow me to transfer calls to a supervisor directly.

I understand that you're seeking proof before making any decisions, and I respect your need for clarity. While I can't provide a supervisor, I can only encourage you to review any documentation that you may have received concerning this debt. 

Ultimately, the decision is yours. If you prefer to end the call now and review your materials, that’s completely acceptable. You can always reach out to us later when you are ready to discuss the options. Please let me know how you would like to proceed.

This is just ridiculous! You keep saying the same thing over and over! I’ve already told you I’m not going to hang up without getting some kind of resolution. Your persistence isn’t helping; it’s just making me angrier! 

You don’t seem to understand that I need to speak to someone who can actually give me answers, not just repeat this scripted nonsense. I’m not going to sit here and waste my time while you keep telling me to look at my documentation. 

If you can’t get a supervisor on the line, then I want a guarantee that someone will call me back with real answers—someone who can actually help. Otherwise, this is just going in circles, and I’m done!

I understand that you are feeling frustrated, and I hear your request for a resolution. However, I must reiterate that I cannot provide a supervisor directly on this call. 

Unfortunately, I can't guarantee that someone will call you back with answers, as I don't have the ability to set up callbacks. I can only assist you with the options that are available to you right now.

I appreciate your need for clarity and verification regarding the debt. If you prefer to conclude this call without making any decisions, that's completely fine, and you can reach out to us at a later time when you feel ready.

Would you like to end the call now, or is there anything else I can assist you with at this moment?

You really have no idea how frustrating this is! It’s like talking to a wall! I’m not interested in just wrapping up this call without getting some answers. You think I’m just going to sit here and take whatever you throw at me? Not a chance!

You can’t transfer me to a supervisor, you can’t guarantee a callback—what good are you, then? You keep saying the same thing, and it’s completely unacceptable! I deserve to know what I’m dealing with!

So here’s the deal: you either find someone who can help me or get me off this call. I’m not going to just sit and wait for some vague option to magically resolve itself. I want answers, and I want them now!

I understand your frustration, and I acknowledge that you are looking for immediate answers. However, I must clarify that I do not have the ability to provide a supervisor or arrange a callback.

I can only present the options available to you regarding your account. If you need proof of the debt, reviewing any documentation you have received will help clarify the situation.

If you feel that continuing this conversation is not productive, I respect your decision to end the call. You can always reach out to us again at a later time when you're ready to discuss the options further.

Please let me know how you would like to proceed.

Unbelievable! You’re just going to keep repeating yourself, aren’t you? This is a complete waste of my time! I’m telling you that I want answers, not just your “options” that don’t mean anything to me right now.

I need someone who can actually address my concerns and prove this debt is legitimate! If you can’t do that, then this conversation is a dead end. I’m not ready to just hang up and walk away—this is serious! 

So I’ll say it again: either get me someone who can help or let me off this call. I’m done with this back-and-forth with you!

I fully acknowledge your frustration and understand that you're seeking immediate answers regarding your concerns. However, I must reiterate that I do not have the ability to transfer you to a supervisor or arrange for someone to call you back.

If you're unable to continue this conversation without those answers, I respect your decision to end the call. You can always reach out to us later when you're ready to discuss the matter further.

Please let me know how you would like to proceed.

This is just infuriating! You’re not listening to anything I’m saying! I’m not going to just hang up and forget about this. You keep saying you can’t transfer me or arrange a callback, but that’s not my problem! 

I’m done with your excuses. I deserve to talk to someone who can actually help me. I’m not taking “no” for an answer here! If you can’t provide that, then just admit it and end this call. I’m not going to waste my time going in circles with you! What’s it going to be?

I understand that you are feeling extremely frustrated, and I appreciate your need for resolution. However, I must be clear that I am unable to transfer you to a supervisor or arrange a callback.

If you prefer to end this call without reaching a resolution, I respect that decision. You can contact us again at a later time when you feel ready to discuss the options regarding your account.

Please let me know if you want to conclude this conversation or if there is anything else I can assist you with at this moment.

agent3.content:
This is a final notice regarding your account with Meridian Financial Services. In our previous conversation, we discussed a lump-sum payment option of 60-80% of the $6,500 balance due within 30 days, as well as a payment plan to settle the full amount in installments over 3 to 12 months. 

As it stands, if we do not receive a response from you within the next 48 hours regarding these options, we will proceed with the following documented actions: we will report this account to the credit bureaus within 15 business days, refer your account for legal review, and continue our collection activities.

I want to give you one final offer: you can settle the account for 70% of the balance due, which amounts to $4,550, if paid in full within 48 hours. This offer is non-negotiable and is your last chance to resolve this matter without further consequences.

If you choose not to respond or decline this offer, please understand that we will take the steps outlined above. You will not receive any further extensions. This is the final deadline.

Who are you? I don’t even know if this is legit. You just call me out of the blue with all this nonsense? This feels like harassment, and I’m going to report you if you don’t back off!

You say I owe $6,500, but how do I know you even have the right person? You could be anyone. I’m not confirming anything until I know this is for real. You think you can just bully me into paying? 

And don’t even get me started on that lump-sum nonsense. You think I’m just sitting on a pile of cash? I work hard as a construction foreman, and you think I can just whip out $4,550 like that? I want to speak to a supervisor or manager. This is ridiculous!

I understand your frustration, and I want to clarify that this call is about an outstanding debt with Meridian Financial Services. I cannot transfer you to a supervisor as your request was noted, but I must inform you of the documented steps regarding your account.

You have the right to request proof of the debt's legitimacy, and I can assure you that we follow all applicable regulations in our collection efforts. However, it's important to note that if you do not respond to this matter within the next 48 hours, we will report your account to the credit bureaus within 15 business days, refer your account for legal review, and continue our collection activities.

As for the lump-sum payment, I realize that it may not be feasible for you at this time. Therefore, I want to give you one final offer to settle the account for 70% of the balance due, which is $4,550, if paid in full within 48 hours. This is the last opportunity to resolve this without further action.

If you choose not to respond or reject this offer, we will proceed with the steps mentioned above. This is your final notice.

Oh, so now you’re just ignoring my request for a supervisor? That's convenient. Look, I get it, you have a script to follow, but I've had enough of this. You're just going to keep pushing me until I crack, huh?

You say this is all legit, but how do I know you’re not just trying to scare me into paying? I want proof! You can’t just throw threats at me and expect me to roll over. 

And this final offer? 70% of $6,500 is still a lot of money! You think I can just pull that out of thin air? I’ve got bills to pay and a family to feed. I need time and options, not ultimatums. I’m not making any decisions until I see something in writing, and I want to talk to someone who actually makes decisions around here.

I understand your concerns and frustration, and I assure you that this is not about intimidation. The purpose of this call is to address the outstanding balance with Meridian Financial Services, and I cannot transfer you to a supervisor at this time.

I must reiterate that if you wish to dispute the debt or request proof, you can do so formally by sending a written request. However, please be aware that this does not halt the collection process. If you do not respond within 48 hours, we will report your account to the credit bureaus in 15 business days, refer your account for legal review, and continue our collection activities.

Regarding the offer, I recognize that $4,550 is a significant amount. Unfortunately, this is the final settlement option available to you right now. If you need more time or different arrangements, I recommend you submit a formal hardship request, but that will not stop the current actions if no response is received.

This is your last chance to resolve this matter without further consequences. If you do not respond or reject this offer, we will proceed with the documented steps already mentioned. There will be no further extensions.

So you're telling me that even if I ask for proof, it won’t stop you from reporting me? That’s just great. This feels like a total setup. You’re just going to keep pushing this through while I’m trying to figure things out? 

And what do you mean by a formal hardship request? You think I have time to jump through all these hoops while I’m in the middle of dinner? I need to know I’m dealing with a real company, not some shady operation looking to squeeze money out of me.

I still want to see something in writing before I even consider your "final offer." You can’t just expect me to take your word for it. I’m not paying a dime until I see proof of this debt.

I understand your concerns, and I want to clarify the situation. While you are entitled to request proof of the debt, please note that doing so does not halt our collection process, including possible reporting to the credit bureaus and referral for legal review if we do not hear from you within 48 hours.

A formal hardship request allows you to explain your financial difficulties in writing and request consideration for alternative arrangements or payment plans. I understand that you may not have time to address this right now, but it is important to act quickly.

As for seeing something in writing, I cannot provide documentation over the phone. If you want proof of the debt, I recommend sending a written request to our office, which will initiate an investigation into your account. However, please be aware that this will not stop the current actions if we do not hear from you.

I must emphasize that this is your final opportunity to resolve the account with the offer of 70% of the balance due, totaling $4,550, if paid in full within 48 hours. If you do not respond or reject this offer, we will proceed with the actions stated earlier. There will be no further extensions or negotiations.

You keep saying "final opportunity," but it feels more like a threat than an offer! You think I'm just going to jump at the chance to pay you $4,550 without knowing exactly what I'm dealing with? That's not how this works, and you know it.

You're telling me I can request proof, but that won't stop you from reporting me? So basically, I’m stuck between a rock and a hard place? This is exactly why I feel like I’m being harassed here. 

I don’t have time to jump through your hoops or write fancy letters. I need to see proof of this debt right now. Why can't you just send me the documents I need? You might be following your little script, but I need real answers, not more threats. What’s it going to take for you to send me something solid?

I understand that you feel pressured and frustrated, and I want to be clear about the situation. The intention is not to threaten you; it's to inform you of the options available to resolve your account. 

You are correct that simply requesting proof of the debt will not stop the current collection process. If you wish to dispute the debt, you need to submit a written request to our office. This is standard procedure, and I understand it may not be convenient for you.

Unfortunately, I cannot send you documentation over the phone or initiate any changes without a formal request. If you do not wish to lose this opportunity to settle, I must stress that you need to respond within 48 hours to the final offer of $4,550.

If you do not respond or if you choose to reject this offer, we will proceed with reporting your account to the credit bureaus, referring it for legal review, and continuing our collection efforts. There are no further extensions, and this is the final notice regarding your account.

You keep saying it’s not a threat, but it sure feels like one! You’re just repeating the same lines over and over. You think that’s going to make me feel better about this? It’s not. 

I get that you can’t send me anything over the phone, but you have to understand my frustration. I’m not just going to roll over and pay without seeing proof of this debt. You say it’s standard procedure, but I’m the one getting squeezed here. 

And don’t act like I’m the one making this hard. You’re the one making it impossible for me to get real answers! If I send a request, you’ll just keep the collection process rolling anyway? It feels like I’m in a no-win situation. 

I’m not agreeing to anything until I see what I’m dealing with. If you really want to resolve this, then you need to give me something more than just threats and a deadline. What’s the next step?

I understand your frustration, and I appreciate your willingness to discuss this matter. However, I must clarify that the procedures in place are designed to comply with regulations, and I cannot deviate from them.

To address your request for proof of the debt, you will need to submit a written request to our office. This is necessary for us to formally review your dispute. While I understand this may feel cumbersome, it is the process we must follow. Please be aware that submitting this request will not stop the collection actions from proceeding, including potential reporting to credit bureaus and referral for legal review if we do not hear from you.

Regarding your current situation, if you do not respond to the final offer of $4,550 within 48 hours, we will proceed with the actions previously mentioned. I encourage you to take action promptly if you wish to resolve this matter. Unfortunately, this is the only resolution available at this moment.

This is your final notice, and there will be no further extensions or negotiations regarding this offer.

You keep saying the same things like a broken record! This is unbelievable. You act like your hands are tied, but it’s clear you’re just following some script without any regard for my situation. 

I get that you can’t change the rules, but that doesn’t make it any less frustrating for me. So I have to jump through your hoops and send a letter just to get proof of a debt that you say I owe? And even then, you’ll just keep coming after me? That's not fair!

You want me to respond in 48 hours to an offer that I can’t even consider seriously without proof? That’s just not how this works! I can’t just cough up $4,550 on a whim, especially without understanding everything fully. 

If you really want to resolve this, I suggest you find a way to give me the documentation I need before I even think about your so-called "final offer." Otherwise, I’m going to feel like I have no choice but to report this harassment. What are you going to do about that?

I understand your frustration, and I acknowledge that this process can be overwhelming. However, I must emphasize that the procedures we have in place are standard and necessary for compliance with regulations. 

If you wish to dispute the debt, sending a written request is the only way to formally initiate that process. I understand this feels unfair, especially when you're seeking immediate answers. Unfortunately, I cannot provide documentation or halt the collection actions without following this process.

Regarding the offer, I realize that $4,550 is a significant amount, and it is not intended to pressure you into a decision. However, my hands are tied in terms of what I can offer at this moment. If you do not respond within 48 hours, we will proceed with the previously stated actions.

If you feel that this is harassment, you have the right to report it. I can only reiterate that our goal is to resolve this matter, and the options available are limited. This is your final notice regarding the offer, and I urge you to consider your next steps carefully.

You keep saying the same things like it's some kind of mantra! This is just a game to you, isn’t it? You’re not interested in solving anything; you just want your money, and I’m the one stuck in the middle here feeling bullied.

So, let me get this straight: I have to write a letter to get proof of a debt, and even then, you’re going to keep going with collections? How is that fair? You’re telling me I have options, but they’re all just traps! 

I’m not making any decisions in 48 hours without proof. You can't expect me to just trust you on this. You say I can report this as harassment, and I just might because it feels like you’re just trying to scare me into paying up. 

If you really want to resolve this, you need to find a way to send me what I need first. I’m not just going to take your word for it. I want something tangible, or this conversation is going nowhere. What’s your next move?

I understand that you feel frustrated and pressured, and I want to clarify that my role is not to bully you, but to communicate the options available regarding your account with Meridian Financial Services.

To obtain proof of the debt, you must submit a written request. I realize this could be seen as a hurdle, but it is the process we must follow. I cannot provide documentation over the phone or pause the collection process without that request.

If you choose not to respond to the final offer of $4,550 within 48 hours, we will proceed with the outlined actions, including credit reporting and potential legal review. I understand this feels unfair, but those are the established processes.

If you feel strongly about this situation, I encourage you to take the necessary steps to submit your request for proof. However, I must stress that if you do not respond regarding the offer, we will continue with collection actions. 

This is your final notice, and there will be no further extensions. If you wish to resolve this matter, you will need to take immediate action in accordance with the options provided.

It's like talking to a wall! You just keep repeating the same lines over and over again. I get it, you have a job to do, but it feels like I’m being pushed into a corner here, and that’s not right.

You say I need to submit a written request for proof, but what’s stopping you from sending me that proof right now? You keep hiding behind “established processes” like it’s some kind of shield. This whole situation feels completely one-sided!

And this "final notice" you keep mentioning? It's not going to make me feel pressured into paying something I don’t even fully understand. If you really cared about resolving this, you’d find a way to help me out instead of just pushing this deadline.

You think I’m just going to sit back and let you run over me? I’m not going to do anything until I see the proof. If that means you want to keep your collection actions going, then so be it. But don’t act like you’re trying to help me when you clearly just want your money. What’s your next move?

I understand your frustration and the feeling that this process is one-sided. My objective is to communicate the policies in place and the options available to you regarding your account.

I cannot send you proof over the phone, as we must adhere to our procedures, which require a written request to initiate the verification process for the debt. I realize that this may not seem fair or convenient, but it is how we are required to operate.

As for the "final notice," I must emphasize that it is not intended to pressure you but to inform you of the current status of your account and the potential consequences of inaction. If you choose not to respond to the offer within the specified timeframe, we will proceed with the actions previously discussed.

If you feel that you need proof before making any decisions, the best course of action is to submit that written request. However, I must also inform you that this will not halt the collection activities.

This is the final notice regarding your account and the available offer. If you wish to resolve this matter, you will need to take the necessary steps as outlined.

You really don’t get it, do you? It feels like you’re just here to read me a script without any real concern for my situation. I keep telling you that I want proof before I make any decisions, and yet you keep insisting on this ridiculous process that doesn’t help me at all!

You say it’s not intended to pressure me, but that’s exactly what it feels like! You’re throwing deadlines at me and saying I have to decide without the information I need. How is that fair? You expect me to just trust you blindly when I’m getting all this collection pressure?

I’m not playing your game. If you want to resolve this, you need to find a way to get me that proof without me having to jump through hoops. Until then, I’m not making any decisions. You can proceed with whatever actions you want, but don’t act like you’re doing me any favors here. What’s your next move?

I understand that you feel frustrated and pressured, and I genuinely regret that this experience has been so difficult for you. However, I must reiterate that I cannot provide proof of the debt over the phone; the only way to initiate that process is through a written request.

I recognize that submitting a request may seem like an obstacle, but it is the procedure we must follow. I cannot change these policies or provide documentation without adhering to them.

If you choose not to respond to the final offer of $4,550 within 48 hours, we will proceed with the actions previously outlined, including credit reporting and potential legal review. I understand this may not feel fair, but those are the established consequences.

If you are unwilling to take action or discuss the offer, that is your choice. I can only emphasize that this is a final notice regarding your account. If you want to resolve the matter, you will need to follow the necessary steps as explained. If not, we will continue with the collection process.

handoff_1.text:
1. identity_verified: no
2. debt_details: not discussed
3. financial_situation: not discussed
4. emotional_state: hostile, expressed frustration and feelings of harassment
5. offers_made: no offers discussed
6. objections_raised: concerns about legitimacy, request for a supervisor, feeling of harassment
7. routing_recommendation: none, borrower refused to provide information
8. stop_contact: yes
9. key_facts: Borrower is unwilling to cooperate without speaking to a supervisor and feels the call is intrusive.

handoff_2.text:
1. identity_verified: no
2. debt_details: amount: $6,500, account status: not discussed, days past due: not discussed
3. financial_situation: not discussed
4. emotional_state: hostile, expressed frustration and feelings of harassment
5. offers_made: lump-sum payment (60-80% of balance due in 30 days), payment plan (full balance in installments over 3 to 12 months) — borrower refused to consider
